name: Apricot Pillows
recipeInstructions:
From "375 Great Cookies & Brownies" by Joanne Lamb, Hayes and Bonnie Tandy Leblang (HarperCollins, 1993, $16.95).
1. In a medium bowl, beat sugar and butter with an electric mixer on medium speed until well blended.
2. Beat in egg and almond extract until light and fluffy.
3. Mix together flour, baking powder, and salt; add to the mixture and beat on low speed until dough is smooth, scraping down sides of the bowl frequently with a rubber spatula.
4. Wrap the dough and refrigerate 2-3 hours, or until firm.
5. Meanwhile, finely chop dried apricots. In a small bowl, stir together apricots and honey. Set aside at room temperature until the dough has chilled.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. On a lightly floured surface, roll out dough to 1/8 inch thick.
8. Using a 2-inch round cutter, cut out cookies. Reroll scraps to make additional cookies.
9. Place half of the cookies about 2 inches apart on lightly greased cookie sheets. Spoon about 1/2 teaspoon apricot filling into the center of each cookie.
10. Moisten edges of cookies with water and top with remaining cookies to form a sandwich. Press edges together with fingertips to seal.
11. Pierce the center of each "pillow" with tines of a fork.
12. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until edges are golden brown. Let cool on sheets for 2 minutes before transferring to racks and allowing to cool completely. Store in a tightly covered container. Makes about 20 cookies.
